c004.q: What is the sum of the infinite geometric series 3 + 1 + 1/3 + 1/9 + вҖҰ?
c004.exp: Sum = a/(1вҲ’r) = 3/(1вҲ’1/3) = 3/(2/3) = 9/2. The series converges because |r| = 1/3 < 1. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c004.q: The 15th term of the arithmetic sequence 7, 11, 15, вҖҰ is:

opts: Show P(1) is true, assume P(k), then derive P(k+1)
Show P(1) and P(2) are true
Show P(n) works for all even n
c004.exp: Induction: (1) Base case P(1) вң“. (2) Assume P(k), show P(k+1) follows. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c004.q: A bank earns 6% annual interest compounded monthly. The monthly rate used in A = P(1+r/n)^(nt) is:
c004.exp: r/n = 0.06/12 = 0.005. Compounding monthly means 12 periods per year. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c004.q: The Fibonacci sequence F(1)=1, F(2)=1, F(n)=F(nвҲ’1)+F(nвҲ’2). What is F(7)?
c004.exp: F(3)=2, F(4)=3, F(5)=5, F(6)=8, F(7)=13. Each term is the sum of the two preceding. [TExES 235 Comp 004]
1+3+5+7+9 = 25. This equals nВІ = 5ВІ = 25, the sum of the first 5 odd numbers. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c004.q: An infinite geometric series converges if and only if:
c004.exp: ОЈarвҒҝ converges iff |r| < 1. If |r| вүҘ 1, partial sums diverge. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c004.q: Sum of first n terms of an arithmetic series with first term aвӮҒ and last term aвӮҷ:
c004.exp: Arithmetic series sum: SвӮҷ = n/2В·(aвӮҒ+aвӮҷ), derived by pairing first and last terms. [TExES 235 Comp 004]

c007.q: What is the domain of f(x) = (x+2)/(xВІвҲ’4)?
c007.exp: xВІвҲ’4 = (xвҲ’2)(x+2) = 0 when x = Вұ2. Both excluded from domain. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: The function f(x) = (3xВІ+1)/(xВІвҲ’1) has a horizontal asymptote at:
c007.exp: Equal degree numerator and denominator: horizontal asymptote = ratio of leading coefficients = 3/1 = 3. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: y = |x вҲ’ 3| вҲ’ 2 compared to y = |x| is:
c007.exp: y = |xвҲ’h| + k shifts y=|x| right h and up k. Here h=3, k=вҲ’2: right 3, down 2. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: The slant asymptote of f(x) = (xВІ+3x+1)/(x+1) is:
c007.exp: Long division: (xВІ+3x+1)Г·(x+1) = x+2 remainder вҲ’1. Slant asymptote: y = x+2. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: Which function has a vertical asymptote at x = 4 and a hole at x = вҲ’1?
c007.exp: A hole occurs when a factor cancels; asymptote when it doesn't. (x+1) cancels вҶ’ hole at x=вҲ’1; (xвҲ’4) remains вҶ’ asymptote at x=4. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: For piecewise f(x) = {xВІ if x<0; 2x+1 if xвүҘ0}, f(вҲ’2) + f(3) =

c007.exp: Swap x and y: x=(2yвҲ’1)/(y+3). Solve: x(y+3)=2yвҲ’1; y(xвҲ’2)=вҲ’3xвҲ’1; y=(3x+1)/(2вҲ’x). [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c007.q: A rational function's graph has a hole when:
opts: The numerator degree exceeds the denominator
A factor cancels from both numerator and denominator
c007.exp: A hole (removable discontinuity) occurs when a common factor cancels from numerator and denominator. The function is undefined at that x-value. [TExES 235 Comp 007]

c008.exp: logвӮӮ(x(xвҲ’2))=3 вҶ’ x(xвҲ’2)=8 вҶ’ xВІвҲ’2xвҲ’8=0 вҶ’ (xвҲ’4)(x+2)=0. x=4 (x=вҲ’2 rejected: log requires positive argument). [TExES 235 Comp 008]

c008.q: The graph of y = 3ЛЈ reflected over the y-axis is:
c008.exp: Reflecting over y-axis replaces x with вҲ’x: y=3вҒ»ЛЈ=(3вҒ»В№)ЛЈ=(1/3)ЛЈ. [TExES 235 Comp 008]
logвӮғ(3Ві)=3 uses logвӮҗ(aвҒҝ)=n, the inverse relationship between exponential and logarithmic functions. [TExES 235 Comp 008]

c008.q: Population of 500 grows at 4% continuously. After 10 years (P = PвӮҖeКібө—):
Earthquake A is 1000Г— more intense than B. How much greater is A's Richter magnitude?
c008.exp: M = log(I/IвӮҖ). Difference = log(1000) = 3. Logarithmic scales compress large ranges. [TExES 235 Comp 008]

c008.q: Which models a quantity whose rate of change is proportional to its current amount?
c008.exp: If f'(t) = kВ·f(t), then f(t) = CeбөҸбө—. Defining property of exponential growth/decay. [TExES 235 Comp 008]
logвӮ„(64/4) = logвӮ„(16) = logвӮ„(4ВІ) = 2. Quotient rule of logarithms. [TExES 235 Comp 008]

c008.q: $10,000 at 5% continuous interest. Years to double? (ln 2 вүҲ 0.693)

c009.q: Coordinates on the unit circle at angle 2ПҖ/3:
c009.exp: 2ПҖ/3 is in QII. Reference angle ПҖ/3: cos(2ПҖ/3)=вҲ’1/2, sin(2ПҖ/3)=вҲҡ3/2. [TExES 235 Comp 009]

c009.q: y = 3sin(2x вҲ’ ПҖ/2) has amplitude, period, and phase shift:
c009.exp: Amplitude=|A|=3. Period=2ПҖ/B=2ПҖ/2=ПҖ. Phase shift=C/B=(ПҖ/2)/2=ПҖ/4 right. [TExES 235 Comp 009]

c009.q: sinВІx + cosВІx вҲ’ 1 simplifies to 0 using:
c009.exp: Pythagorean identity: sinВІx+cosВІx=1, so the expression = 1вҲ’1 = 0. [TExES 235 Comp 009]
arctan returns values in (вҲ’ПҖ/2, ПҖ/2). tan(вҲ’ПҖ/4)=вҲ’1, so arctan(вҲ’1)=вҲ’ПҖ/4. [TExES 235 Comp 009]
y=AВ·sin(2ПҖft) вҶ’ 2ПҖf=880ПҖ вҶ’ f=440 Hz. This is the musical note A4. [TExES 235 Comp 009]
sin(x)=cos(x) вҶ’ tan(x)=1 вҶ’ x=ПҖ/4+nПҖ. In [0,2ПҖ): x=ПҖ/4 and x=5ПҖ/4. [TExES 235 Comp 009]

c009.q: Vertical asymptotes of y = tan(x) occur at:
c009.exp: tan(x)=sin(x)/cos(x) undefined where cos(x)=0, i.e., x=ПҖ/2+nПҖ for integer n. [TExES 235 Comp 009]

c010.exp: Factor: (x+2)(xвҲ’2)/(xвҲ’2) = x+2. As xвҶ’2, limit = 4. [TExES 235 Comp 010]

c010.q: f'(x) > 0 for x < 3 and f'(x) < 0 for x > 3. At x = 3, f has a:
c010.exp: First derivative test: f' changes positiveвҶ’negative at x=3 вҶ’ local maximum. [TExES 235 Comp 010]
вҲ«2x dx + вҲ«3 dx = xВІ + 3x + C. Always include constant of integration C. [TExES 235 Comp 010]

c010.q: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: if F'(x) = f(x), then вҲ«(a to b) f(x)dx =
c010.exp: FTC Part 2: вҲ«(a to b) f(x)dx = F(b)вҲ’F(a). Connects differentiation and integration. [TExES 235 Comp 010]

c010.q: Particle position s(t) = tВі вҲ’ 6tВІ + 9t. Particle is at rest when:
c010.exp: At rest: v(t)=s'(t)=3tВІвҲ’12t+9=3(tвҲ’1)(tвҲ’3)=0 вҶ’ t=1 and t=3. [TExES 235 Comp 010]

c010.q: f''(x) < 0 on an interval means f is:
c010.exp: f''(x)<0 вҶ’ concave down (opens like вҲ©). f''(x)>0 вҶ’ concave up (opens like вҲӘ). [TExES 235 Comp 010]
вҲ«2x dx = xВІ. Evaluate: [xВІ] from 1 to 3 = 9вҲ’1 = 8. [TExES 235 Comp 010]

c013.q: A chord of length 8 is 3 units from the center. The radius is:
c013.exp: Perpendicular from center bisects chord. Half-chord=4, distance=3. rВІ=4ВІ+3ВІ=25 вҶ’ r=5. [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c013.q: An inscribed angle intercepting a semicircle measures:
c013.exp: Inscribed angle = half the intercepted arc. Semicircle = 180В° arc вҶ’ inscribed angle = 90В°. (Thales' theorem) [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c013.q: Area of a sector with radius 6 and central angle 120В°:
Arc length with radius 9 and central angle 2ПҖ/3 radians:
Two tangent segments from an external point to a circle are always:
c013.exp: Tangent segments from an external point are equal in length. Fundamental theorem of circle geometry. [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c013.q: Two chords AB and CD intersect inside a circle at P. AP=4, PB=9, CP=6. Then PD=
c013.exp: Intersecting chords theorem: APВ·PB = CPВ·PD вҶ’ 4В·9 = 6В·PD вҶ’ PD = 6. [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c013.q: A tangent line to a circle at point P is:
opts: Perpendicular to the radius at P
Parallel to the radius at P
c013.exp: A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c013.q: A composite figure: rectangle 8Г—5 with a semicircle (diameter 5) on top. Total area:
c013.exp: Rectangle = 40. Semicircle: r=2.5, area=ПҖ(2.5)ВІ/2=6.25ПҖ/2=25ПҖ/8вүҲ9.82. Total=40+25ПҖ/8. [TExES 235 Comp 013]

c014.q: Equation of circle with center (3, вҲ’2) and radius 5:
Point A(2,5) reflected over y = x is:
c014.exp: Reflection over y=x swaps coordinates: (a,b)вҶ’(b,a). A(2,5)вҶ’A'(5,2). [TExES 235 Comp 014]

c014.q: Dot product of u = (3, 4) and v = (1, вҲ’2):
c014.exp: uВ·v = (3)(1)+(4)(вҲ’2) = 3вҲ’8 = вҲ’5. Negative вҶ’ obtuse angle between vectors. [TExES 235 Comp 014]
4xВІвҲ’9yВІ=36 вҶ’ xВІ/9вҲ’yВІ/4=1. Standard hyperbola form (opposite signs). [TExES 235 Comp 014]

c014.q: Dilation from origin with scale factor 3 maps (2, вҲ’1) to:

c014.exp: Vertex form y=a(xвҲ’h)ВІ+k has vertex (h,k). Here vertex=(1,3). [TExES 235 Comp 014]

c014.q: Translation 5 right and 3 down maps (x, y) to:
c014.exp: T(h,k): (x,y)вҶ’(x+h, y+k). Right 5 вҶ’ h=+5; down 3 вҶ’ k=вҲ’3. Image: (x+5, yвҲ’3). [TExES 235 Comp 014]

c015.exp: n=7, median is the 4th value = 9. [TExES 235 Comp 015]
IQR = Q3вҲ’Q1 = 35вҲ’20 = 15. Measures spread of the middle 50% of data. [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c015.q: In a right-skewed distribution, the relationship between mean and median is:
c015.exp: Right skew: tail pulls mean toward larger values. Mean > Median > Mode is typical. [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c015.q: Best graph for the distribution of a large continuous data set:
c015.exp: Histograms display frequency distribution of continuous data, showing shape, center, spread, and outliers. [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c015.q: Adding 10 to every data value changes which measures?
opts: Mean and median shift by 10; SD unchanged
Mean, median, and SD all increase by 10
c015.exp: A linear shift adds to measures of center (mean, median). Measures of spread (SD, IQR) are unchanged. [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c015.q: r = вҲ’0.92 between study time and test errors indicates:
opts: Strong negative: more study вҶ’ fewer errors
c015.exp: |r| near 1 = strong linear relationship. r < 0 = negative (as study time increases, errors decrease). [TExES 235 Comp 015]

opts: The mean more than the median
The median more than the mean
c015.exp: The mean uses all values mathematically вҖ” extreme values pull it significantly. Median is resistant to outliers. [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c015.q: Which measurement scale allows only classification (no ordering)?
c015.exp: Nominal: data are labels/categories with no natural order (e.g., eye color, type of car). [TExES 235 Comp 015]

c019.q: A teacher asks students to represent area of a circle as a function of diameter. This demonstrates:
opts: Using multiple representations of a mathematical concept

c019.q: вҲҖx вҲҲ в„қ, xВІ вүҘ 0 translates verbally as:
opts: For all real numbers x, x squared is вүҘ 0
There exists a real x such that xВІ вүҘ 0
c019.exp: вҲҖ = 'for all'; вҲҲ в„қ = 'in the real numbers'. [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: A student explains exponential decay models radioactive half-life. This illustrates:
opts: Connecting mathematics to science and real-world contexts
c019.exp: Comp 019: understanding how mathematics models and solves problems in other disciplines. [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: Geometric probability is a ratio of two:
c019.exp: Geometric probability = area of target region / total area. Key multiple-representation connection in Comp 019. [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: 'The area under the velocity-time curve represents displacement' illustrates:
opts: A connection between calculus and physics via the definite integral
c019.exp: Comp 019: вҲ«v(t)dt = displacement connects calculus to physics (mathematics used in other disciplines). [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: A teacher says '2/3 means 2 of 3 equal parts, 0.667, 66.7%, and 2Г·3.' This demonstrates:
opts: Multiple representations of a rational number
c019.exp: Comp 019: communicating mathematical ideas using numeric, verbal, visual, and symbolic representations. [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: Using a table, graph, and equation to teach linear functions demonstrates:
opts: The rule of multiple representations in mathematical communication
c019.exp: Using tabular, graphical, and algebraic representations is central to Comp 019. [TExES 235 Comp 019]

c019.q: Which best describes using graphs, tables, and diagrams to communicate mathematical information?
opts: Visual media as mathematical communication tools
c019.exp: Comp 019: understanding the use of visual media (graphs, tables, diagrams, animations) to communicate mathematical information. [TExES 235 Comp 019]
